fields.description:
## Why
The auto-lander is a long-running service; it must deploy like the review-bot and log to its OWN service logs ‚Äî writing to `session_log` would blur that type's purpose (it is for agentic sessions, not CI/infra processes).

## What
Containerize the service and wire it into docker-compose (single instance) on the existing Gerrit box; route logs to container stdout ‚Üí the existing CloudWatch monitoring.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S2** (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) ‚Äî the core loop: this story packages and deploys the loop service, so there must be a service to containerize; the deploy is otherwise independent of the failure/command stories.

## Scope
Deploy + observability + single-instance guard. Not the loop logic.

## Files
New files:
- `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` ‚Äî service image + `HEALTHCHECK`.
- `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf` ‚Äî the `autolander_errors` alarm.

Existing files edited:
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` ‚Äî add the `autolander` compose service (single instance).
- `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` ‚Äî add the `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` SecureString param.
- `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` ‚Äî fetch the autolander Gerrit token at boot.
- `infra/scripts/observability.sh` ‚Äî add the `autolander_errors` `PutMetricData` feeders.

Read-only / unchanged (referenced, not edited): `infra/terraform/iam.tf` ‚Äî its existing `ssm_params_read` grant on the `rebar/prod/*` prefix already covers the token path, so NO IAM change is needed. Operator cutover (not a repo file): the `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` capability grant (`rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` + submit on `refs/heads/*`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` (new) + a compose service added to the existing `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`.
- [ ] **Single-instance guard:** the container acquires a POSIX advisory **file lock** (`flock` on a lockfile in its state volume) on start; a second copy fails to acquire it and exits. This `flock` is the **sole** single-instance mechanism ‚Äî do NOT rely on `deploy.replicas: 1` (a Swarm-only key silently ignored by plain `docker compose`).
- [ ] **Credentials (AC2 mechanism):** `autolander-bot`/RebarBotNava's `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` is a **server-level capability** granted in `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` (`[capability]` / service-user grant), not a per-project label ACL; plus submit permission on `refs/heads/*`. The Gerrit HTTP token is provisioned as an SSM **SecureString** at the concrete path `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` ‚Äî inside the existing `parameter/rebar/prod/*` prefix that the EC2 instance-role IAM policy (`iam.tf` `ssm_params_read`) already grants, so NO IAM change is needed (a `/rebar/autolander/*` path would fall outside that grant) ‚Äî added in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, fetched by `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`; never inlined.
- [ ] **Observability (AC3):** logs go to container stdout; `infra/scripts/observability.sh` publishes the single `rebar/host:autolander_errors` metric (`PutMetricData`) from BOTH feeders ‚Äî the `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` journald marker AND the container's `unhealthy` state (grepped on `CONTAINER_NAME=compose-autolander-1`) ‚Äî one metric, two feeders, with an alarm in `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf`. The service NEVER writes `session_log`.
- [ ] **Liveness/restart (AC4):** a `HEALTHCHECK` in `Dockerfile.autolander` marks the container `unhealthy` on a **hang** (a plain `restart: unless-stopped` only fires on process EXIT, not a hang); the compose service + an autoheal/`restart: unless-stopped` recreate it on exit/unhealthy, and the `unhealthy` state feeds the same `autolander_errors` metric named in AC3. (The `wipChain`-timeout parking of a stuck stack is loop logic owned by S2/S3, not this deploy story.)
- [ ] **Heartbeat (AC5):** the bot writes a **heartbeat** ‚Äî a timestamp file in the state volume (or a health metric) ‚Äî on **every poll** iteration, so a stale heartbeat means the bot is down/wedged. This heartbeat (its agreed location + a staleness threshold) is the interface contract S4's `lander_down` outcome reads.
- [ ] **Queue-status surface (AC6):** emit a small queue-status surface exposing the current `wipChain` (change, phase, `tested_sha`, time-in-phase), the count of `Autosubmit`-set-and-waiting changes, and a **queue-wait / time-to-land histogram** metric ‚Äî for debuggability and to validate the success criterion.
- [ ] **Emergency-stop + drain (AC7):** an **emergency-stop** sentinel (a sentinel file / config flag the poll loop checks) transitions the bot to a `paused` state that STOPS consuming `Autosubmit` (for when `main` is on fire). **Drain-on-shutdown:** on redeploy/SIGTERM, finish the current `wipChain`, ack its outcome, then exit ‚Äî so a redeploy never strands a half-processed stack.
